name: Artichokes and Peas
recipeIngredient: Large Basil Leaves - sliced into strips
1 Large Onion, white or yellow - sliced 1/4-inch thick
1 pound Fresh Pod Peas OR 1 cup Frozen Peas
1 tablespoon Sweet Butter OR Extra-Virgin Olive Oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the upper 2/3 of the leaves off the artichokes, then break off the remaining leaves, snapping them off at the base. Trim off the dark green stems, going around the artichoke with a paring knife.
2. Cut the artichokes in quarters, remove the fuzzy choke and slice each quarter into pieces 1/4-to-1/2-inch thick. As you work, rub the cut surfaces with lemon and place them in a bowl with lemon juice and water to cover.
3. Gently warm the olive oil with half the basil in a pan.
4. When the oil is fairly hot but not sizzling, add the onions and the sliced artichokes. Salt lightly and stir to coat them with the oil.
5. Add 3/4 cup water and cook over a medium-low flame until the artichokes are cooked through, about 25 minutes.
6. As the water cooks off, add more in 1/2-cup increments until the artichokes are done. Add the peas and continue cooking until they are cooked through.
7. Let any remaining liquids reduce until they form a syrupy consistency. Taste and season with salt.
8. Add the remaining basil, parsley, butter or olive oil, and stir in a little lemon juice or champagne vinegar to taste.
